title: Signal engineering is one of the last powerful levers in paid UA
insight: Marketers have three main levers: creative assets, budget/bidding, and signal (the data you share with ad networks). While creative gets most attention, signal is rarely discussed despite its significant impact. Designing what data you share with networks—rather than just sending all subscriptions—can significantly improve performance.
raw_excerpt: Marketers have three main levers for campaigns: creative assets, budget/bidding, and signal (the data you share). While creative gets most attention, signal is one of the last powerful levers we have but is rarely discussed despite its significant impact on performance.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Audit what conversion events you currently send to Meta
Map your full funnel from install to paid subscription and identify which events correlate most with business value
Evaluate if your current optimization event truly aligns with actual revenue outcomes

title: Always optimize for events that align with actual business goals, never clicks or installs
insight: Platforms literally give you what you optimize for. Optimizing for clicks yields cheap clicks that don't convert. Optimizing for installs yields installs that don't convert to trials. The optimization event you choose directly determines the quality of traffic the algorithm seeks out.
raw_excerpt: Ad platforms literally give you what you optimize for. If you optimize for clicks, they'll give you cheap clicks that don't convert. If you optimize for installs, they'll give you installs that don't convert to trials. Always optimize for events that align closely with your actual business goals.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Switch from install optimization to at minimum trial start optimization
If you have enough volume, test optimizing for subscription purchase or qualified trial
Verify in Meta Ads Manager that your campaigns are set to maximize app events, not installs

title: Target 10+ conversion events per day per campaign for stable algorithm learning
insight: The fundamental tradeoff in signal optimization is volume vs. quality. Even with small budgets, you can usually optimize deeper than installs. Aim for at least 10 events per day per campaign. If you can't reach this volume, increase budget, change the conversion event, or don't run the campaign.
raw_excerpt: The fundamental tradeoff in signal optimization is volume versus quality. Even with small budgets, you can usually optimize for events deeper in the funnel than installs. Aim for at least 10 events per day per campaign for stable performance while maximizing quality.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Check each campaign's daily conversion event count over the past 7 days
If under 10/day, consider moving to a slightly higher-funnel event that still correlates with value
Consolidate campaigns that individually lack volume rather than running many underfunded ones

title: Use qualified trial events to filter low-quality users from your signal
insight: When optimizing for trials, Meta often delivers young users who start free trials but cancel quickly because they're cheap to acquire. Use data breakdowns to identify these patterns, then either exclude in targeting (e.g., 25+) or create a 'qualified trial' event that only fires for users meeting quality criteria based on age, onboarding answers, or cancellation timing.
raw_excerpt: When optimizing for trials, platforms like Meta often deliver young users who start free trials but cancel quickly because they're cheap to acquire. Use data breakdowns to identify these patterns, then either exclude in your targeting or use a 'qualified trial' event.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Analyze trial-to-paid conversion by age bracket in your product analytics
Identify which segments have disproportionately low conversion (typically 18-24)
Build a qualified trial event that excludes or delays signal for low-converting segments
Test sending the qualified event to Meta instead of raw trial starts

title: Filter negatively (remove worst users) rather than positively (target only best)
insight: Instead of filtering positively for your best users (which limits volume too much for the algorithm), filter negatively by removing your worst users from the signal. This maintains enough volume while improving quality. Figure out which onboarding questions help you identify and filter out low-quality users.
raw_excerpt: Instead of filtering positively for your best users (which limits volume too much), try filtering negatively by removing your worst users. This maintains enough volume for the algorithm while improving quality.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Identify the bottom 20-40% of trialers by downstream conversion and find common traits
Build negative filters based on onboarding answers, device OS, or age
Withhold the trial event for these segments rather than trying to only send signal for top users

title: Use onboarding questions to identify high-fit users for qualified signal
insight: Think about questions you ask during users' first session (within 24 hours) that indicate which users are a better fit—such as urgency of their problem or use case. If these answers materialize in conversion numbers, use them to define your qualified event. Career-related motivations typically convert much higher than leisure-related ones.
raw_excerpt: Think about the questions you could ask during users' first session (within 24 hours) that are likely to indicate which are a better fit (e.g., urgency of problem). If you see this materialize in conversion numbers, then you can use that for your 'qualified' event.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Analyze trial-to-paid conversion by each onboarding question answer
Identify which answers predict high vs low conversion
Build conditional logic: only fire the qualified trial event if user answers indicate high fit
Validate that the qualified event still provides sufficient volume (10+/day)

title: Age is the strongest predictor of trial conversion in subscription apps
insight: In consumer subscription, trial conversion is highly correlated with age. Downstream trial CVR by age can range dramatically: 18-24 at 18%, 25-34 at 26%, 35-44 at 32%, 45-54 at 44%, 55-64 at 48%. Ads that attract older users are more valuable and need different cost per trial goals. Use value rules/multipliers rather than full exclusion.
raw_excerpt: Age is often a strong predictor of trial conversion. If you find users below a certain age have poor trial-to-paid conversion, either filter them out of your trial optimization campaigns or create separate campaigns for younger users optimized for different events with appropriate bid levels.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Pull trial-to-paid conversion rates by age bracket from your product analytics
Set up Value Rules in Meta to modify bids by age based on actual conversion data
Consider separate campaigns for under-25 with lower CPA targets if you want to serve them
Use the age-restricted goods toggle to enforce 25+ minimum if young users consistently don't convert

title: Be cautious filtering out immediate auto-renewal cancellers from signal
insight: Many users who immediately cancel auto-renewal might actually convert later, possibly on different subscription products. What appears to be low-intent behavior might indicate savvy users who eventually become high-value customers. Check your data to see to what extent early cancellers bring revenue before filtering them out.
raw_excerpt: Be cautious when filtering out users who immediately cancel auto-renewal. Many of these users might actually convert later, possibly on different subscription products. What appears to be low-intent behavior might actually indicate savvy users who eventually become high-value customers.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Analyze what percentage of early cancellers eventually generate revenue
If filtering, determine the right cancellation window (e.g., 10 min vs 10 hours)
Ensure you're filtering enough to matter—Marcus suggests checking thresholds like 10% at 10min vs 30% at 10hr

title: Start with AEM on Meta rather than SKAN, use Meta SDK over third parties
insight: For app promotion on Meta, start with AEM (Aggregated Event Measurement) rather than SKAN. AEM doesn't have SKAN's drawbacks: delayed event arrival, privacy thresholds that hide data. Avoid using too many third parties as it makes setup more complex and error prone. The Meta SDK is often the best choice for cleaner signal.
raw_excerpt: On Meta, start with AEM rather than SKAN, especially if you're at a small scale. AEM doesn't have the same drawbacks as SKAN: delay in events arriving, privacy thresholds, etc. It's best to avoid using too many third parties as it makes the setup more complex and error prone.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Verify AEM is properly configured in your Meta Events Manager
If currently using SKAN only, add AEM as primary attribution method
Avoid mixing SDK and MMP signal sources to prevent deduplication issues
Check that Meta SDK events fire correctly with proper parameters before layering in CAPI

title: Avoid mixing signal sources (SDK + MMP) early on
insight: Mixing signal sources—like using both an SDK and MMP simultaneously—often leads to deduplication issues, filtering problems, and messy data that can significantly impact campaign performance. Especially in early stages, pick one source and stick with it.
raw_excerpt: Avoid mixing signal sources (like using both an SDK and MMP simultaneously) especially in early stages. This often leads to deduplication issues, filtering problems, and messy data that can significantly impact campaign performance.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Audit your current signal setup: are you sending events from both SDK and MMP to Meta?
Choose one primary signal source per campaign type and disable the other
If using an MMP, verify its deduplication logic with the platform SDK

title: Meta SDK attributes more events than CAPI for app promotion campaigns
insight: Through incrementality tests, Marcus has found that CAPI attributes fewer events compared to using Meta SDK for app promotion campaigns. For app campaigns, SDK events are preferred over CAPI when the web definition allows. Meta doesn't report a 'match rate' for app events like they do for web events.
raw_excerpt: You can optimize an app promotion campaign on Meta and optimize for conversion API events (CAPI), but through incrementality tests Marcus has found for his clients that there are less CAPI events attributed vs. using the Meta SDK.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: For App Promotion campaigns, prefer SDK events over CAPI for optimization
Reserve CAPI for Web Sales campaigns where it excels
Run your own incrementality test comparing SDK vs CAPI attribution if at sufficient scale

title: Run both app and web campaigns on Meta to de-risk and access different audiences
insight: Having a campaign optimizing for web events (Sales campaign with CAPI) alongside app event campaigns de-risks your setup. You can shift budgets if algorithm changes occur—like when Meta over-targets Facebook Reels on app campaigns. Different campaign types access different algorithmic pretraining and audience pools.
raw_excerpt: Having a campaign optimizing for web events (Sales campaign with CAPI to send back signal) in addition to campaigns optimizing for app events derisks your setup because you can shift budgets in case of algorithm changes.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Set up a Web Sales campaign with CAPI alongside your existing App Promotion campaigns
Ensure CAPI event match quality is high in Events Manager
Monitor placement distribution differences between app and web campaigns
Be prepared to shift budget between campaign types if one starts underperforming

title: Use multiple optimization events across campaigns to address a bigger market
insight: It's not about finding one perfect optimization event—it's about utilizing events in different ways. You might have 2 campaigns optimizing for the same qualified trial but with different CPA targets by audience segment, or use campaigns with entirely different events to attract different audiences that convert on different timelines.
raw_excerpt: There can be value in having multiple events different campaigns optimize for to broaden who you're acquiring and address a bigger market: some might convert and monetize quickly, other over the long run.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Map out which conversion events address which audience segments
Set separate CPA targets for campaigns targeting older vs younger demographics
Experiment with different optimization events for web vs app campaigns
Consider a purchase-optimized campaign alongside a trial-optimized one

title: Use creative testing campaigns with the same optimization event as scaled campaigns
insight: In most cases, your creative testing campaign should optimize for the same event as your scaled BAU campaign. Only use a different event (e.g., install) if you've validated that it attracts the same audience—not just in demographics but also behavior. Same validation applies across geos.
raw_excerpt: In most cases, have your creative testing campaign optimize for the same event as your scaled campaign. Only use a different event if you validated that you can attract the same audience, not only in terms of demographics but also behavior.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Set your creative testing campaign to the same optimization event as BAU
If you must use a higher-funnel event for testing, validate audience overlap first
Compare age, gender, device, and placement breakdown between test and BAU campaigns

title: Check placement and demographic evolution when testing new optimization events
insight: As you test new optimization events, monitor the evolution of placements and demographics in Meta (especially age distribution) to verify you're actually targeting and acquiring the higher-quality audience you intended. This is your feedback loop to confirm signal changes are working.
raw_excerpt: As you test new optimization events, also check the evolution of placements and demographics in Meta (e.g., age) to see if they do indicate you're targeting and acquiring the higher quality audience you're going after.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Set up Ads Reporting with pivot table: ad name × age × placement × spend
Compare demographic and placement breakdown before and after signal changes
If age distribution doesn't shift toward higher-converting segments, re-examine your qualified event definition

title: For web funnels you MUST use CAPI—the pixel alone won't capture all signal
insight: If you have a funnel/quiz with purchases on the web, you must use Meta's Conversion API because the Meta pixel won't capture all the signal. Check your event match quality in the Events Manager and potentially send additional signal to help attribution.
raw_excerpt: If you have a funnel/quiz with purchases on the web you need to be using Meta's Conversion API because the Meta pixel won't capture all the signal. Check your event match quality in the event manager.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Implement CAPI for all web funnel conversion events
Check Event Match Quality score in Meta Events Manager—aim for Good or Great
Send additional parameters (email, phone, fbp, fbc) to improve match quality
Compare pixel-only vs CAPI event counts to quantify the gap

title: Send events for all media sources including organic to give platforms more signal
insight: In MMP integrations with ad networks, make sure you send events for 'All media sources, including organic.' You're giving more signal to the platform to optimize—even non-converters provide useful negative signal. If you filter to attributed-only, you're relying on the MMP's organic definition.
raw_excerpt: In the ad network integrations within your MMP, make sure you send events for 'All media sources, including organic' because you're giving more signal to the platform to optimize.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Check your MMP's ad network integration settings
Switch event sharing to 'All media sources, including organic'
Understand that you then need to decide how much you trust ad network vs MMP attribution

title: For Google Ads, Firebase events unlock value-optimized campaigns and ODM
insight: It's not clear if Firebase events vs MMP events provide an optimization advantage for Google Ads campaigns. However, features like value-optimized campaigns and the upcoming ODM (on-device management) only work with Firebase events, making them strategically important.
raw_excerpt: For Google Ads, it's not clear if there's an advantage in using Firebase events vs. MMP events for campaigns in terms of actual optimization. However there are features like value-optimized campaigns and soon ODM that only work with Firebase events.
source_title: How to optimize your ad campaigns with signal engineering (RevenueCat Webinar)
actionable_steps: Ensure Firebase events are properly set up even if using an MMP for primary attribution
Test value-optimized campaigns on Google Ads using Firebase events
Monitor ODM availability and prepare to adopt when it launches

title: Verify Events Manager data before doing anything in Ads Manager
insight: Before trying to get smart with advanced signal engineering, verify that your basic event data is actually being shared properly with correct parameters. What's in Events Manager must be correct before you work on anything in Ads Manager. Garbage in, garbage out.
raw_excerpt: 'Garbage in, garbage out'. Before you try to get smart with advanced events, verify that this data is actually shared properly, with the according parameters. You want what's in the Events Manager to be correct before you do anything in the Ads Manager.
source_title: Signal engineering bootcamp (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Audit Events Manager for every conversion event you optimize for
Verify event parameters are populated correctly (value, currency, content_id)
Check for deduplication issues between SDK and CAPI events
Fix Events Manager issues before touching Ads Manager campaign settings

title: Beware of free trial signal when a high percentage don't convert to paid
insight: A trial is not a paid subscription. If a high percentage of free trials don't convert, optimizing for trial starts sends misleading signal. Optimize for a conversion signal that truly correlates with business value, happens early enough (day 0), and occurs often enough (10+ per day per campaign).
raw_excerpt: Beware of the convenience of 'free trials' when a high % don't convert: a trial is not a paid subscription. Optimize paid campaigns for a conversion signal that truly correlates with business value. This signal should happen early enough (day0) and often enough (10+/day/campaign).
source_title: Signal engineering bootcamp (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Calculate your current free trial to paid conversion rate
If it's below 30-40%, strongly consider using a qualified trial or purchase event instead
Ensure whatever event you choose fires within 24 hours and at 10+/day volume

title: Value-based campaigns are powerful but require variance and scale
insight: Value-based campaigns have various benefits: they help with scale, are less binary than event-count optimization, and can reach different audiences. But beware—they can also be more volatile, require higher scale to stabilize, and are useless without enough variance in the values you send.
raw_excerpt: Value based campaigns have various benefits (scale, less binary, reach different audiences...) but beware that they can also be more volatile, require higher scale, and are useless without enough variance in value.
source_title: Signal engineering bootcamp (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Analyze the variance in your subscription values—if everyone pays the same, value optimization won't help
Consider pricing variance (weekly, monthly, annual) as a way to create value differentiation
Only test value-based optimization when you have sufficient scale (well above 10 events/day)
Monitor volatility closely in the first weeks after switching

title: Onboarding must bridge the gap from dopamine feeds to product value
insight: Users from Meta aren't searching for a solution—they're bored scrolling. Your onboarding is a sales funnel: amplify the problem, prove credibility, create an AHA moment, and offer multiple price points. Targeting broadly means converting broadly. Think of onboarding as the extension of the social feed. Multiple offers for multiple audiences.
raw_excerpt: Onboarding must bridge the gap from dopamine feeds to product. Users from Meta aren't searching for a solution, they're bored scrolling. Your onboarding is a sales funnel: amplify the problem, prove credibility, create an AHA moment and offer multiple price points.
source_title: Unifying Meta, ASO & onboarding for scale (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Map your onboarding flow against these stages: problem amplification, credibility, AHA moment, offer
Design the first 3 screens as a continuation of the ad experience, not a product tour
Offer multiple price points/subscription lengths to convert different intent levels
Test storytelling intros that mirror social feed content before jumping into quiz questions

title: Google Ads success is driven by inventory identification, not just creative volume
insight: Advertisers obsess over high-volume creative testing to find winners and try to apply that same playbook to Google. But on Google, success is driven just as much (if not more) by identifying the right inventory. Focusing on earlier indicators like CPM and traffic quality can accelerate learnings and lead to more efficient acquisition than simply cycling through creatives.
raw_excerpt: Advertisers obsess over high-volume creative testing to find winning assets and often try to apply that same playbook to Google. But on Google, success is driven just as much (if not more) by identifying the right inventory.
source_title: How to launch, test, and scale Google Ads for your app (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Analyze CPM and traffic quality across Google's inventory types (Search, YouTube, Display, Discover)
Identify which inventory placements drive actual downstream conversions
Focus optimization efforts on inventory signals before scaling creative volume
Use placement reports to understand where Google is spending your budget

title: Android acquisition on Google can be significantly more cost-efficient at scale
insight: Many subscription apps overlook Android because iOS users are traditionally higher value. But acquiring Android users (particularly on Google) can be significantly more cost-efficient. When scaled, the unit economics of Android acquisition can be extremely attractive because Google owns the full stack on Android.
raw_excerpt: Many subscription apps prioritize iOS because those users are traditionally higher value. However, this often leads them to overlook Android. In reality, acquiring Android users (particularly on Google) can be significantly more cost-efficient.
source_title: How to launch, test, and scale Google Ads for your app (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Calculate your Android LTV vs CPA ratio and compare to iOS
Launch Google App campaigns targeting Android if not already running
Consider that Google owns Play Store search + YouTube + Display on Android, giving them better signal
Don't dismiss Android just because per-user LTV is lower—total economics may be better

title: 2025 is about creative diversity over creative volume due to Andromeda
insight: 2024 was about creative volume. In 2025, with Meta's Andromeda update, the shift is to creative diversity. But with AI image and video generation models improving rapidly, neither volume nor diversity will be constraints much longer. The real value will be in knowing what ads to create and how to prioritize creative concepts.
raw_excerpt: 2024 was about creative volume. In 2025, with Andromeda it's all about creative diversity. The problem is going to be that we have way too many creatives and not enough ad budgets to test them. The real value is going to be in knowing what ads to create.
source_title: How to scale Meta with dozens of AI agents (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Shift creative testing focus from volume to diversity of concepts and formats
Invest in creative strategy and concept prioritization frameworks
Use AI tools for production speed but focus human effort on hypothesis generation
Diversify across creative types (statics, UGC, animation, screen recordings) to serve Andromeda

title: Recruit your own users as TikTok/Instagram UGC creators
insight: Whether free or paid, your users can be your creators. Put banners on your website calling for creators and call out for creators within your app. Apps like FocusFlight and Opal have successfully embedded creator recruitment directly into their product experience.
raw_excerpt: Whether they're free or paid, your users can be your creators: Put a banner on your website calling for creators. Call out for creators within your app.
source_title: Stealth marketing — how to scale with TikTok and Instagram UGC networks (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Add a 'Become a Creator' banner to your website
Build an in-app callout for users to create content (after they've had a positive experience)
Create a simple creator brief template users can follow
Test using this user-generated content in paid Meta and TikTok campaigns

title: Web-to-app funnels expand reach to older high-converting demographics on Meta
insight: Web-to-app funnels serve as an audience expansion strategy to reach older demographics with higher purchasing power that you can't effectively target through app campaigns. Web2app placement distribution on Meta skews more toward Feed, which naturally reaches an older audience. Use it as a scaling solution when app campaigns hit CPA inflation.
raw_excerpt: Use web-to-app funnels as an audience expansion strategy, particularly to reach older demographics with higher purchasing power that you can't effectively target through traditional app campaigns.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Launch a Web Sales campaign alongside existing App Promotion campaigns
Use static creatives for web campaigns to push delivery toward FB Feed placements
Compare age distribution between your app and web campaigns
Deploy web funnels specifically when app campaign CPAs start inflating

title: Use different creative strategy for web2app vs app campaigns if audiences differ
insight: If your web2app audience skews older than your app campaign audience, you may need a different creative strategy. Consider showing older creators in web2app creatives. Change the CTA from 'Download' to 'Sign up online' to set correct expectations. Strong creatives should still be tested across both.
raw_excerpt: You might want to have a different creative strategy for web2app vs. app campaigns if the audiences are different. Consider adapting some creatives—for example, you could show older creators in creatives for web2app.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Analyze age/demo breakdown of your web vs app campaigns
Create creative variants with older UGC creators for web campaigns
Change CTA copy to 'Sign up online' or 'Start your plan' for web campaigns
Still test top-performing creatives across both campaign types as a baseline

title: Use web funnel content itself as ad creative for full-funnel consistency
insight: Take the web funnel content itself and test it as an ad creative to create a coherent experience in terms of messaging and visual style. This becomes even more impactful when you deliver real value in the quiz that you can pitch in the ad—like Rise Science showing your sleep debt.
raw_excerpt: You can take the web funnel content itself and test it as an ad to create a coherent experience full-funnel in terms of messaging and visual style.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Screen-record your web funnel and test it as a video ad
Highlight the value moment (e.g., personalized results) in the ad to drive curiosity
Ensure visual style and messaging match between ad and funnel landing page
A/B test funnel-content ads vs standard UGC/static ads

title: Test quiz-specific messaging in creatives; the first funnel screen matters most
insight: Test quiz-specific messaging in your ad creatives to improve the full experience. The most important screen of your web2app funnel is the first one, so start your optimization there. Over time this may lead to having many different funnel flows matched to different creative messages.
raw_excerpt: Test quiz-specific messaging in creatives to improve the experience even further. The most important screen of your web2app funnel is the first one, so start there.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Match your ad messaging to the first screen of your web funnel
Use UTM parameters to customize the first funnel screen based on which ad drove the click
Prioritize optimizing your funnel's first screen over deeper screens
Test different first-screen variations tied to different creative angles

title: Avoid free trials in web funnels—use paid trials or direct subscriptions
insight: Free trials in web funnels have high cancellation rates and payment failures. Use paid trials or direct subscriptions instead to ensure genuine user commitment and reduce fraud. Paid trials also provide more signal volume for Meta to optimize and a cheaper cost per purchase for creative testing.
raw_excerpt: Avoid free trials in web funnels due to high cancellation rates and payment failures. Use paid trials or direct subscriptions instead to ensure genuine user commitment and reduce fraud.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Replace free trials with paid trials ($2.99-4.99/week or month) in your web funnel
Consider direct quarterly subscriptions for web funnels
Use discounted offers as a fallback for users who don't convert on the initial paywall
Monitor that paid trial signal volume is sufficient for Meta's learning phase

title: Web funnels need stronger trust-building because they lack app store credibility
insight: Web onboarding must do an even better job than app onboarding since it lacks app store credibility signals (ratings, reviews, download counts). Technical-only flows won't convert cold traffic—you need to build trust and demonstrate value before asking for conversion. Create an 'aha moment' before the paywall through assessments, mini-products, or personalized insights.
raw_excerpt: Web onboarding must do an even better job than app onboarding since it lacks app store credibility. You need to build trust and demonstrate value before asking for conversion.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Add trust signals throughout your web funnel: testimonials, review scores, press logos, user counts
Deliver genuine value before the paywall (personalized assessment, score, or preview)
Don't just adapt your app onboarding—rebuild for web with trust-building as the primary goal
Study successful web funnels in your vertical (Noom, Rise Science, Ahead) for structure

title: Web campaigns compete with eCommerce CPMs and take longer to exit learning phase
insight: With web campaigns on Meta, you compete with eCommerce advertisers, not just other apps. This can drive higher CPMs. Additionally, web campaigns take longer to exit the learning phase because conversion events (paid trials, direct purchases) are more expensive than app installs, meaning fewer events per day.
raw_excerpt: Keep an eye on CPM trends, because with web campaigns you compete with eCommerce and not just apps anymore. Web campaigns take longer to exit the learning phase because conversion events are more expensive.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Monitor CPM trends for web campaigns vs app campaigns
Budget for a longer learning phase with web campaigns (potentially 2-3x app campaign timelines)
Consider using paid trial events (higher volume, lower cost) vs purchase events to accelerate learning
Be patient with initial web campaign performance—don't kill too early

title: Test big swings first in web funnels, not small messaging changes
insight: Don't start web funnel experiments with small messaging changes because you won't have enough data to make a decision. Test big swings that can materially affect conversion rate and ARPU: major pricing changes, removing or adding entire sections, fundamental paywall redesigns.
raw_excerpt: Don't start your web funnel experiments by testing smaller messaging changes because you won't have enough data to make a decision. Test big swings that can affect conversion rate and ARPU.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Start web funnel testing with major structural changes: different pricing, section removal, paywall redesign
Save headline and copy tweaks for later when you have a working baseline
Ensure each test variant is different enough to produce statistically significant results quickly
Prioritize tests by expected impact on ARPU and conversion rate

title: 20%+ of web subscribers never open your app—design the post-purchase bridge
insight: Over 20% of web subscribers never open your app after purchasing. Build a good post-purchase experience: use an MMP deferred deeplink on top of on-page instructions and an email summarizing next steps. Ideally the App Store page shown is a Custom Product Page, and users land directly on a login screen.
raw_excerpt: 20%+ of web subscribers never open your app after purchasing. Use an MMP deferred deeplink on top of on-page instructions and an email summarizing next steps.
source_title: Web2App Quizzes: From Side Project to Profit Engine (FunnelFox)
actionable_steps: Measure your web purchase → app open rate
Implement MMP deferred deeplinks for web-to-app handoff
Send an immediate email after web purchase with clear app download/login instructions
Create a Custom Product Page for web funnel users to land on in the App Store
Ensure the app login flow is frictionless for web subscribers

title: Two-step creative testing: validate delivery first, then test conversion
insight: Implement a two-step creative testing process. Step 1: validate which ads Meta wants to spend on—use minimal budget with many creatives (e.g., $100 with 50 creatives), separating statics and videos. Step 2: test conversion performance on the top performers with more budget (e.g., 5 winners). This prevents wasting budget on ads that won't get delivery.
raw_excerpt: First, validate on which ads Meta wants to spend. Use a minimal budget and a lot of creatives together. Then, test conversion performance on the top ones with more budget. This approach prevents wasting budget on ads that won't get delivery.
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: Set up a low-budget creative test ad set with 30-50 new creatives
Separate statics and videos into different ad sets
After 48-72 hours, identify which ads received meaningful spend
Graduate top 3-5 spenders into a higher-budget conversion test
Use the same optimization event in both steps as your BAU campaigns

title: Reframe creative testing as rapid loser elimination, not winner identification
insight: The fastest path to finding winning creatives is systematically and quickly removing the ones that don't work. This frees resources to focus on promising candidates. You can pause an ad within an hour or two to kill non-performers quickly, then continue culling as you look at progressively deeper funnel metrics.
raw_excerpt: Reframe creative testing as rapid loser elimination rather than winner identification. The fastest path to finding winning creatives is systematically and quickly removing the ones that don't work.
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: Set clear kill criteria: pause ads with zero spend after 24-48 hours
Progressively filter by deeper metrics: first delivery, then CPC, then CPA
Don't wait for statistical significance on every ad—focus resources on survivors
Build a weekly cadence of launching, killing, and graduating creatives

title: Trust Meta's delivery decisions as a proxy for conversion potential
insight: Meta's decision to show your ads is already a signal of conversion probability. Ads that get delivery have passed the platform's prediction model. Use this as a filter before investing budget in deeper conversion testing. The worst thing you can do is force spend on every single ad.
raw_excerpt: Trust Meta's delivery algorithms as a proxy for conversion potential. The platform's decision to show your ads is already a signal that they have conversion probability. The worst you can do is force spend on every single ad.
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: Don't look at creatives before launching—anonymize to remove emotional bias
Let Meta's delivery signal be your first filter before examining conversion data
Never force equal spend across all ads in a test—let the algorithm allocate
Only review creative content after you've identified delivery patterns

title: Give niche-audience creatives their own ad set when they can't compete with winners
insight: Ads that show promise in creative testing (spend traction, decent CAC) but don't get delivery when placed alongside winners in the main ad set may need their own space. If the content targets a specific or different audience, create a separate ad set. Otherwise, keep them in backlog for when the current winner starts fatiguing.
raw_excerpt: Ads promising in creative testing that don't get much delivery once added in the main ad set alongside winners might need space in a specific ad set (especially if content shows they target a specific/niche/different audience).
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: When a tested creative shows good CAC but loses delivery to winners, check its audience profile
If it targets a different demographic/placement, give it a dedicated ad set in BAU
If it targets the same audience, add it to a backlog for rotation when winners fatigue
Set different CPA targets for niche-audience ad sets based on their expected downstream value

title: Consider placement restrictions when statics can't compete with video for spend
insight: If static ads don't get any spend against videos in the same ad set, consider restricting placements by creative type: Feed-only for statics and Reels-only for video. This forces delivery on the placement where each format is native, rather than letting video always win the auction.
raw_excerpt: In the case where statics don't get spend vs. videos, consider restricting placements for specific creative types (e.g., Feed for static and Reel for video).
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: Separate statics and videos into different ad sets (aligned with Burke's deconsolidation approach)
If statics still don't spend, test Feed-only placement restriction for the static ad set
Monitor whether placement restriction improves or harms overall CPA
Use this as a diagnostic tool to understand if statics can perform on their natural placements

title: Demographic targeting is mostly irrelevant—use creatives and signal instead
insight: Demographic targeting beyond age is largely irrelevant. Even for age, it's better to use a value rule multiplier rather than fully excluding demographics. Instead: (1) use creatives for targeting—different creative types naturally reach different demographics, and (2) filter the signal you send back to the network if you want less of a specific segment.
raw_excerpt: Demographic targeting is largely irrelevant, besides age. But even then, it's best to use a multiplier/value rule rather than fully exclude. Use creatives for targeting and filter out the signal you're sending back.
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: Remove interest-based and most demographic targeting from your campaigns
Set up Value Rules for age brackets based on actual trial-to-paid conversion data
Use creative type (static vs UGC video) to naturally reach different demographics
Filter signal (qualified events) rather than restricting targeting to control audience quality

title: AI creative production is about speed and volume, not cost savings
insight: Producing more and faster is more important than producing cheaper. Cost savings is not the main driver behind AI creative production. People testing 5 ads are now testing 50, and people testing 50 are testing 2,000. Within 6 months, AI videos will clearly be the standard production method.
raw_excerpt: Producing more and faster is more important than producing cheaper. Cost savings is not the main driver behind using AI to produce creatives. People that were testing 5 ads are now testing 50, and people that were testing 50 are testing 2000.
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: Invest in AI creative tools to increase testing velocity, not just reduce costs
Set a target to 5-10x your current creative testing volume
Build a workflow where AI handles production while humans focus on concept strategy
Test AI-generated video ads alongside human-produced ones to benchmark quality

title: Use AI for forming creative hypotheses, not just production
insight: AI can be used to form test hypotheses and craft differentiated creative concepts, not just for actual production. There might even be an advantage in not always producing via AI, because AI-produced videos may not stand out. The real value of AI will be for the insights you can't see on your own.
raw_excerpt: AI can be used to form test hypotheses and craft differentiated creative concepts, not just for the actual production. There might even be an advantage in not always producing via AI.
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: Use AI to analyze competitor ads and identify concept patterns
Feed performance data and user research into AI to generate creative hypotheses
Mix AI-produced and human-produced creatives in your testing pipeline
Consider that AI-looking creatives may have lower engagement due to oversaturation

title: AppLovin and gaming ad networks are viable for non-gaming subscription apps
insight: It's not uncommon for non-gaming subscription apps to allocate 20-25% of budget to AppLovin. But there are opportunities beyond AppLovin on other 'gaming' ad networks. Evaluate whether expanding to a new channel is more efficient than increasing spend on existing ones by considering the diminishing returns curve.
raw_excerpt: It's not uncommon for non-gaming and subscription apps to have 20-25% of their budget going to AppLovin. But there are opportunities beyond AppLovin on 'gaming' ad networks.
source_title: MDM Mailbag #3 (Mobile Dev Memo Podcast S6E1)
actionable_steps: If not already running AppLovin, evaluate it as a scaling channel
Map your ROAS curves by channel to identify where marginal spend is most efficient
Test other ad networks beyond AppLovin (Unity, ironSource) for incremental reach
Compare new channel CPA vs incremental CPA on existing channels before expanding

title: Validate messaging on statics before scaling to video production
insight: First validate which messaging works by testing headlines across different categories (general, testimonial, pain-point, desire-based, loss aversion, selfish unspoken desires) on a few static ad formats. Once you identify winning headlines, expand to other creative formats including video. This is more capital-efficient than starting with expensive video production.
raw_excerpt: First, you need to validate messaging. Come up with headlines in different categories: general, testimonial, pain-point, desired-based, loss aversion, selfish unspoken desires. Test all of these headlines on a few different static ad formats.
source_title: Creative Testing Strategy Masterclass in 2025
actionable_steps: Write 10-15 headlines across the 6 messaging categories
Pick 2-3 static formats you're confident in (or that have worked before)
Launch a test campaign with all headline × format combinations
Once you identify 3-5 winning headlines, produce video creatives using those messages

title: Mine Meta ad comments and customer reviews for creative angles
insight: Go through your Meta ads' comments and use them for creative angles. Look at what people are commenting—both positive reactions and objections. Use this to create UGC mashups, address objections in new creatives, and identify messaging that resonates organically. Also leverage customer reviews from app stores and Reddit.
raw_excerpt: Go through your Meta ads' comments and try to come up with a UGC mashup based on them. Look at what people are commenting and use that for your creative angle. This also helps you understand what people's objections are.
source_title: Creative Testing Strategy Masterclass in 2025
actionable_steps: Review comments on your top-spending ads in Meta Ads Manager
Categorize comments by: praise, objections, questions, use cases
Create creatives that directly address the top 3 objections
Build UGC-style mashups from real user language in comments and reviews

title: Use JTBD interviews as input for creative concepts and messaging
insight: Leverage jobs-to-be-done interviews to figure out motivation, triggers, deciding factors, and USPs. Use these insights alongside customer reviews (TrustPilot, app store reviews) to generate ad concepts. Founders and team members close to customers are a gold mine—interview them to get prioritized creative ideas.
raw_excerpt: Leverage jobs to be done interviews to figure out motivation, triggers, deciding factors, USPs. Use this as input along with customer reviews to generate ad concepts.
source_title: Creative Testing Strategy Masterclass in 2025
actionable_steps: Conduct 5-10 JTBD interviews with recent subscribers
Extract triggers, motivations, and deciding factors from interviews
Map these to messaging categories for systematic creative generation
Interview your founders/customer-facing team for quick hypothesis prioritization

title: Two stages of creative iteration: minor tweaks then concept transfer
insight: Stage 1: take the exact same winning creative and make minor changes—different headline, different product shown, different color. Stage 2: apply the psychological concept or messaging angle to a completely different format. For video, a no-brainer iteration is giving the winning script to another creator with same demographics and similar personality.
raw_excerpt: Stage 1: take the exact same creative and make minor changes to it like changing the headline. Stage 2: apply the psychological concept to a different format.
source_title: Creative Testing Strategy Masterclass in 2025
actionable_steps: For each winner, immediately queue 3-5 Stage 1 variants (headline, CTA, visual tweaks)
If Stage 1 produces results, plan Stage 2: same concept in different creative format
For UGC winners, reshoot with 2-3 different creators using the exact winning script
Track which iteration stage yields higher hit rates to inform resource allocation

title: Use leading indicators to spot potential winners before conversion data arrives
insight: When testing, a creative isn't always an immediate winner. Use leading indicators to understand if there's potential, particularly for purchase-optimized campaigns: cost per click, cost per unique landing page view, cost per initiate checkout, cost per email signup. Also use Meta-specific early metrics: impression-to-engagement rate, impressions-to-3s-video-play, video-plays-to-link-click rate.
raw_excerpt: Use leading indicators to understand if there's potential: cost per click, cost per unique landing page view, cost per initiate checkout. Impressions to post engagement rate, impressions to 3-second video play, and video plays to link click rate are all early indicators.
source_title: Creative Testing Strategy Masterclass in 2025
actionable_steps: Identify the leading indicator patterns from your known winners (CPC, CTR, hook rate ranges)
Set up custom columns in Meta Ads Manager for these proxy metrics
Use these patterns to spot potential in new creatives before conversion data is statistically significant
Remember: hook rate doesn't matter if your CPA is bad—always validate downstream

title: 80% of creatives should NOT be iterations on the same concept
insight: Don't only iterate on winning ads and never test anything new. 80% of your creatives should not be on the same concept. Balance iteration on proven winners with exploration of entirely new concepts. Also, look at previously turned-off past winners and relaunch them to see if they can work again.
raw_excerpt: Don't only focus on iterating on your winning ads and never testing anything new. 80% of your creatives should not be on the same concept.
source_title: Creative Testing Strategy Masterclass in 2025
actionable_steps: Track what percentage of your weekly creative output is iteration vs new concepts
Maintain a backlog of untested concept directions from user research
Periodically relaunch previously paused winners in fresh ad sets
Aim for roughly 70% iteration / 30% new concepts (Félix Boudreau's ratio)

title: Do comment grooming on your Meta ads regularly
insight: Comment grooming means cleaning out negative comments and replying to positive ones. This impacts social proof visible to future viewers of the ad and can affect both CTR and conversion rate, especially on ads with significant spend accumulating comments over time.
raw_excerpt: Do comment grooming on your ads by: cleaning out negative comments and replying to positive comments.
source_title: Creative Testing Strategy Masterclass in 2025
actionable_steps: Set up a weekly comment review process for top-spending ads
Hide or delete negative/spam comments
Reply to positive comments to boost engagement signals
Use tools like AdManage to streamline comment management at scale

title: Expect a flat period before creative testing wins compound
insight: When scaling creative testing, expect an initial flat period before wins emerge. Once you achieve your first significant creative win, learning compounds rapidly leading to dramatic metric improvements. Top health & fitness apps test about 7,000 ads/year; apps like Impulse or WalkFit test up to 50,000. The hit rate is low, so high-volume testing is a competitive necessity.
raw_excerpt: When scaling creative testing, expect an initial flat period before wins emerge. Once you achieve your first significant creative win, learning compounds rapidly.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: Commit to at least 3-4 months of consistent creative testing before judging the program
Track cumulative learnings alongside performance to see the compounding effect
Benchmark your testing volume against category leaders (7K+ ads/year for H&F)
Build a learning database that captures why each creative won or lost

title: Minor creative variations can produce up to 4x performance differences
insight: Seemingly insignificant details in ad creatives can have outsized impacts on all major metrics—up to 4x difference between similar-looking ads. This underscores why extensive testing of small changes is essential. You cannot predict what will work without testing.
raw_excerpt: Minor creative variations can produce dramatic performance differences - up to 4x between similar-looking ads.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: Never assume two similar creatives will perform the same—always test
When iterating on winners, test subtle variations (background color, image, headline placement)
Track and document which minor changes produced the biggest lifts
Resist the urge to only test 'big swings'—small details matter at the creative level

title: Build a creative matrix: value propositions × creative formats
insight: Create a creative matrix that breaks down ads by value proposition angles and creative formats to systematically identify testing opportunities. This framework helps discover untapped combinations and ensures comprehensive coverage. Use Figma or mind maps to visualize it.
raw_excerpt: Create a creative matrix that breaks down ads by value proposition angles and creative formats to systematically identify testing opportunities.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: List all your value proposition angles (from user research, JTBD, reviews)
List all viable creative formats (static, UGC talking head, screen recording, animation, etc.)
Build a matrix and mark which combinations you've tested vs untested
Prioritize untested high-potential combinations for your next creative sprint

title: Implement a structured weekly creative testing cycle
insight: Run a consistent weekly cycle: launch new creatives, use a short 3-day window to determine winners/losers, analyze results, brainstorm based on learnings, and quickly iterate. This cadence creates a flywheel where each week's insights feed into the next week's development. Run one new concept per ad set with several variations.
raw_excerpt: Implement a structured weekly creative testing cycle where run new creatives (3-day window), analyze results, brainstorm based on learnings, and quickly iterate.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: Set a fixed weekly launch day for new creative tests
Use a 3-day evaluation window to identify winners and losers
Schedule a weekly creative review meeting to extract learnings
Feed learnings directly into the next week's creative brief

title: Maintain 70% iteration / 30% new concept ratio in creative testing
insight: Maintain a balanced creative testing mix with roughly 70% iteration on proven winners and 30% new concept exploration. This ensures you extract maximum value from what works while continuously exploring new territory. Adjust the ratio based on diminishing returns from iterations.
raw_excerpt: Maintain a 70% iteration/30% new concept ratio in your creative testing mix.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: Tag each creative as 'iteration' or 'new concept' in your tracking system
Monitor the ratio weekly and rebalance if it drifts too far in either direction
If iteration hit rate drops below 10%, shift more budget to new concepts
Never let iteration work crowd out new concept exploration entirely

title: Implement granular naming conventions for creative assets
insight: Implement a granular naming convention for creative assets to enable deeper data analysis and pattern recognition. Don't rely on Meta's reporting tools alone—track performance in your own system to maintain historical data and uncover long-term insights. But be careful: too-granular naming slows teams down and 95% of creatives will fail anyway.
raw_excerpt: Implement a granular naming convention for your creative assets to enable deeper data analysis and pattern recognition. Don't rely on Meta's reporting tools alone.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: Define a naming convention with key modules: concept, format, angle, creator, version
Keep it practical—balance granularity vs speed of implementation
Export and track performance in a spreadsheet or database outside Meta
Train all team members and agencies on the convention and enforce compliance

title: Apply winning ad copy directly to your onboarding and web funnels
insight: Apply winning ad copy directly to your product onboarding flow to create messaging consistency from acquisition to activation. This can significantly improve conversion rates. For web funnels, use UTM parameters to automatically adapt the onboarding copy to match the specific ad that drove the user.
raw_excerpt: Apply winning ad copy directly to your product onboarding flow to create messaging consistency from acquisition to activation. Use UTMs to adapt automatically the copy to your top winning creatives.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: Identify your top 3 ad copy messages by performance
Test placing winning headlines on the first screen of your onboarding
For web funnels, implement UTM-based dynamic copy that mirrors the ad message
Measure the impact on install-to-trial conversion rate

title: When you find a winning format, apply it across different situational contexts
insight: When you find a winning creative format, systematically apply it across different situational contexts relevant to your audience. This structured iteration can yield multiple winners that outperform the original. Also refresh fatiguing ads by keeping what works but slightly modifying presentation—e.g., a tweet → tweet on a street pole → video of the street pole.
raw_excerpt: When you find a winning creative format, systematically apply it across different situational contexts relevant to your audience.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: List 5-10 different situational contexts where your product solves a problem
Apply your winning format template to each context
When a winner starts fatiguing, evolve the presentation (static → motion → full video)
Track which contexts produce the highest hit rates for future prioritization

title: Combine influencer/affiliate management with UGC sourcing for efficiency
insight: Build a lean creative team that's 'creatively obsessed'—constantly analyzing winning ads, discussing failures, monitoring competitors, tracking organic social trends. Combine influencer/affiliate management with UGC relationship management to maximize efficiency. Finding high-quality UGC requires dedicated relationship management.
raw_excerpt: A good suggestion is to combine the influencer/affiliate management with the UGC relationship management to maximize efficiency.
source_title: How to 10X Your Creative Testing Without Losing Your Mind (MAU Vegas 2025)
actionable_steps: Merge influencer outreach and UGC creator management under one person/function
Build a database of creators who can produce content for both organic and paid
Schedule regular team sessions to review competitor ads and organic trends
Invest in a creative strategist role who bridges performance data and creative production

title: Simple screen recordings can drive viral TikTok growth for early-stage apps
insight: For early-stage app marketing, even simple screen recordings showing your product's core value can drive viral growth on TikTok, especially when you position the product at the intersection of trending technologies or brands that users already understand and love. Create visually distinctive features that stand out from competitors.
raw_excerpt: For early-stage app marketing, even simple screen recordings showing your product's core value can drive viral growth on TikTok.
source_title: Oleve's Michael Giardino: Distribution by NewForm E06
actionable_steps: Record your app's most visually impressive or unique feature as a screen recording
Post it on TikTok with minimal editing, positioned against trending topics
Build features that are visually shareable by design (e.g., BoldVoice's Accent Oracle)
Test using these organic screen recordings as paid ad creatives on TikTok and Meta

title: Invest heavily in first 3 App Store screenshots—95% of users only see those
insight: 95% of potential users only see your first App Store screenshots, making them disproportionately important despite the difficulty of distilling your product into a few words. The investment in getting these right is worth it because they directly impact paid UA conversion rates.
raw_excerpt: Invest heavily in your first three App Store screenshots despite the difficulty to 'boil down your product to four words'. 95% of potential users only see your first screenshots so it's worth it.
source_title: Launched Podcast live (RAGA '25)
actionable_steps: Treat first 3 screenshots as your highest-priority conversion asset
A/B test screenshot messaging and visual design using App Store experiments
Ensure screenshots communicate core value in 4-5 words per screen
Align screenshot messaging with your top-performing ad creative messages

title: Web onboarding prices should differ from in-app—use quarterly, skip trials
insight: Web onboarding prices should be different from in-app prices. Drop free trials entirely on web and use quarterly subscriptions instead. Judge web campaigns differently: they may be unprofitable for the first 3 months and then show a huge ROAS bump at D91 when the quarterly subscription renews.
raw_excerpt: Web onboarding prices should be different from your in-app prices. Forget about trials, and say welcome to quarterly subscription. You may be unprofitable for the first 3 months and then suddenly have a huge ROAS bump at D91.
source_title: Cracking web2app (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Create a separate pricing structure for web funnels (not a copy of in-app pricing)
Test quarterly subscriptions as the default web offer
Extend your ROAS evaluation window for web campaigns to at least D91
Model expected revenue including renewal revenue before declaring a web campaign unprofitable

title: Mini landing pages are the low-hanging fruit of web-to-app
insight: Mini landing pages are the simplest, fastest entry point into web-to-app. They're easy to launch, fast to test, and often the first step to rebuilding efficiency when app campaigns plateau. You don't need a full quiz funnel to start benefiting from web traffic.
raw_excerpt: Mini landing pages are the low-hanging fruit of W2A. They're simple to launch, fast to test, and often the first step to rebuilding efficiency when your app campaigns plateau.
source_title: Cracking web2app (RAGA '25 Workshop)
actionable_steps: Build a simple landing page with your core value proposition and a single CTA
Launch a Web Sales campaign on Meta pointing to this landing page
Test against your app campaigns to see if web traffic is incrementally valuable
Iterate from mini landing page toward a full quiz funnel once you validate the channel

title: Find your attribution multiplier via incrementality testing
insight: Calculate your multiplier by running an incrementality test: turn off Meta ads in a representative market, measure baseline trial volume, turn ads back on, and measure uplift. The formula is: incremental trials divided by Meta-attributed trials equals your multiplier. This tells you how much of Meta's claimed attribution is actually incremental. Keep everything else stable during the test to avoid confounding results.
raw_excerpt: Find your multiplier (SKAN & AEM specific): Run an incrementality test in a representative market. Check your baseline trial volume, turn on campaigns, monitor uplift. Make sure you keep everything else stable. Incremental trials / Meta-attributed trials = multiplier.
source_title: Meta attribution 101. Why not to trust AEM.
actionable_steps: Select a representative market (similar LTV, CPMs, audience composition) for testing
Pause all Meta ads in that market for 2+ weeks to establish baseline trial volume
Turn Meta ads back on and measure the uplift in trial starts
Calculate multiplier: incremental trials / Meta-attributed trials
Apply this multiplier to adjust Meta-reported CPA across all markets

title: Use WDYHAU (Where Did You Hear About Us) surveys to validate attribution match rate
insight: Track the match rate between Meta-attributed conversions and conversions identified via 'Where Did You Hear About Us' (WDYHAU) survey responses in your onboarding. This provides a first-party validation layer on top of Meta's reported attribution. Keep optimizing this match rate over time as a proxy for attribution accuracy.
raw_excerpt: Pro Tip: keep optimizing match rate based on WDYHAU answers. [Chart showing match rate over time between Meta-attributed conversions and Conversions based on WDYHAU]
source_title: Meta attribution 101. Why not to trust AEM.
actionable_steps: Add a 'Where Did You Hear About Us' question to your app onboarding flow
Track the ratio of Meta-attributed conversions to WDYHAU-confirmed Meta users
Monitor this match rate weekly as a proxy for attribution accuracy
Use match rate trends to validate or challenge Meta-reported performance

title: Lookalikes are not dead—most marketers just gave up too early post-ATT
insight: Most subscription app accounts in 2024 are running purely broad targeting, but lookalikes still have value. ATT made lookalikes harder because IDFAs aren't available at scale for custom audiences and other identifiers (email, names) aren't as reliable. Marketers abandoned the strategy because it's hard to execute—but that difficulty is exactly why it's a competitive advantage for those who persist.
raw_excerpt: Why marketers believe Meta lookalikes are dead and why they are wrong. Fast-forward to 2024: Most subscription app accounts are running purely broad...but should they? ATT made it harder to create lookalikes. IDFAs aren't available at scale. Other identifiers aren't as reliable. Marketers abandoned the strategy because it's hard to execute.
source_title: Why marketers believe Meta lookalikes are dead and why they are wrong
actionable_steps: Don't dismiss lookalikes—they still work if you invest in proper data collection and setup
Evaluate whether your current broad-only approach is leaving value on the table
Follow Burke's 7-step framework for a lookalike comeback

title: Lookalikes have higher CPM/CPI but lower CAC—measure ROA not just upper funnel
insight: Comparing lookalikes to broad targeting: lookalikes have higher CPM, CPI, and CPT (upper funnel is more expensive), but produce lower CAC (better downfunnel conversion). Broad targeting has cheaper upper funnel but worse trial-to-paid conversion. Measuring ad set level ROA is tricky with AEM/SKAN, so don't judge lookalikes on CPI alone—evaluate them on actual business outcomes.
raw_excerpt: Also measuring ad set level ROA is tricky with AEM/SKAN. Your upper funnel is typically more expensive, while ROA is stronger. Lookalikes vs Broad: CPM High/Low, CPI High/Low, CPT High/Mid, CAC Low/High.
source_title: Why marketers believe Meta lookalikes are dead and why they are wrong
actionable_steps: Stop evaluating lookalike campaigns solely on CPM, CPI, or CPT
Build downfunnel tracking to measure CAC and ROA per ad set
Compare lookalike vs broad on trial-to-paid conversion rate and lifetime value, not just acquisition cost

title: 7-step framework for lookalike comeback: data, segmentation, seed, narrow, test, analyze, goal
insight: Burke's 7-step lookalike framework: (1) Finetune data collection—get ATT opt-ins and email addresses, plus Stripe/Android data. (2) Segment data for quality and quantity—create 5K+ payer lists, prioritize value over volume. (3) Create seed audiences using 1P data upload (emails, IDFAs in CSV via Meta's template). (4) Create narrow 2% lookalikes for highest quality. (5) Set up test campaign with top creatives and meaningful budget. (6) Analyze downfunnel results—blended metrics, ATT opt-in rate, WDYHAU. (7) Set lookalike CPT goal—expect +20% trial conversion while spending 30% of budget on lookalikes.
raw_excerpt: 7-step framework for your lookalike comeback: 1) Finetune data collection (ATT-optin, email, Stripe, Android) 2) Segment data for quality and quantity (5K+ payers list) 3) Create seed audiences using 1P data upload 4) Create narrow 2% lookalikes 5) Setup test campaign to prove incremental value 6) Analyse downfunnel results 7) Set your lookalike CPT goal.
source_title: Why marketers believe Meta lookalikes are dead and why they are wrong
actionable_steps: Collect ATT opt-ins and email addresses from users to build your seed list
Export 5K+ paying subscribers with emails and IDFAs into a CSV using Meta's template
Upload to Audiences in Meta Ads Manager and create a 2% lookalike
Launch a test campaign with top creatives and meaningful budget—expect higher CPTs
Evaluate on blended metrics, ATT opt-in audience quality, and WDYHAU responses
Set CPT goals factoring in the +20% trial conversion improvement from lookalikes

title: Never treat Meta like a single channel—each placement is its own channel
insight: Meta is not a single channel. FB Feed, FB Video Feed, IG/FB Reels, IG Feed, and Audience Network Rewarded Video are all distinct placements with different audience demographics, content consumption behavior, and performance characteristics. Unlocking one new placement can have a similar effect to adding an entirely new marketing channel to your mix.
raw_excerpt: 1: Never treat Meta like a single channel. [Shows FB Feed, FB Video Feed, IG/FB Reels, IG Feed, AN Rewarded Video as separate channels]. Unlocking one new placement can have a similar effect to adding a new channel to your marketing mix.
source_title: DisAdvantage+: 10 Hot Fixes For Meta's Broken Algorithm
actionable_steps: Pull placement-level breakdowns for all campaigns to understand your current distribution
Identify which placements you're underserving—especially FB Feed and Audience Network
Create creatives tailored for placements you want to unlock (e.g., static images for Feed, video for Reels)
Track performance by placement as if each were a separate channel

title: Understand your traffic composition—older demographics have deeper pockets
insight: Analyze your traffic composition across placements because different placements reach different demographics. Older users (Gen X, Boomers) have significantly more wealth than younger generations (Millennials, Gen Z). For subscription apps, steering your placement mix toward Facebook Feed—which skews older—can access higher-LTV audiences. Use 1P data to model your target placement and demographic mix.
raw_excerpt: 2: Understand your traffic composition. Reminder: old people have deeper pockets. [Generational wealth gap chart showing Baby Boomers at $1.1M vs Millennials at ~$150K]. 3: Model your target mix using 1P data.
source_title: DisAdvantage+: 10 Hot Fixes For Meta's Broken Algorithm
actionable_steps: Pull age and placement breakdowns from your Meta campaigns
Cross-reference with your 1P data to identify which age groups have highest trial-to-paid conversion
Model your ideal placement/demographic mix based on actual LTV data
Steer creative strategy to reach higher-value demographics (e.g., more static/Feed content for older audiences)

title: Creative type equals targeting—structure ad sets by creative format
insight: Your creative type IS your targeting. Different creative formats (static images, UGC video, motion graphics) naturally scale on different placements and reach different demographics. This is why ad sets should be organized by creative type rather than by audience segment. This forced deconsolidation gives you control over your placement and audience mix without restrictive targeting.
raw_excerpt: 4: Creative (type) equals targeting. [Shows creative types mapping to different placements and audiences across FB, IG, Messenger]
source_title: DisAdvantage+: 10 Hot Fixes For Meta's Broken Algorithm
actionable_steps: Separate ad sets by creative type: one for static images, one for UGC video, one for motion graphics
Never mix creative types within the same ad set
Use static creatives to steer toward Feed placements (older demographics)
Use video/UGC creatives to steer toward Reels placements (younger demographics)

title: Burke's manual app ads structure: country tiers, creative types, broad with age exclusion
insight: Burke's scaling structure for manual app ads uses three layers: (1) country tiers grouping similar markets, (2) ad sets split by creative type (static vs video), and (3) broad targeting with young age exclusion (b20+ = age 20+). Naming convention example: AppAds_iOS_BAU_Tier2.0_Broad_b20+_Static. Additional scale comes from Advantage+ campaigns, additional creative types (e.g., Moms-focused), and lookalikes when available.
raw_excerpt: How I structure manual app ads for scale: 1) country tiers, 2) ad sets by creative type, 3) broad targeting w/ age exclusion. Additional scale comes from: 4) Advantage+, 5) additional creative types, 6) lookalikes when available. [Shows ad sets: AppAds_iOS_BAU_Tier2.0_Broad_b20+_Static, AppAds_iOS_BAU_Tier2.0_Broad_b20+_Video, etc.]
source_title: DisAdvantage+: 10 Hot Fixes For Meta's Broken Algorithm
actionable_steps: Group countries into tiers by similar buying metrics and LTV
Create separate ad sets for each creative type within each country tier
Use broad targeting with age exclusion (e.g., 20+ or 25+)
Use naming convention: [CampaignType]_[OS]_[Purpose]_[Tier]_[Targeting]_[AgeMin]+_[CreativeType]
Layer Advantage+ campaigns on top for additional scale
Add lookalike ad sets when you have sufficient 1P data

title: Forced budget diversification and signal quality over quantity
insight: Force budget diversification across placements and campaigns rather than letting Meta consolidate everything into its preferred (often cheapest) inventory. Additionally, prioritize signal quality over quantity—optimize for events that strongly correlate with business value rather than high-volume but low-quality events. These are two of Burke's 10 hot fixes for Meta's algorithm tendencies.
raw_excerpt: 5: Forced budget diversification [across FB, Messenger, IG]. 6: Signal quality over quantity.
source_title: DisAdvantage+: 10 Hot Fixes For Meta's Broken Algorithm
actionable_steps: Split budgets across multiple campaigns targeting different objectives and placements
Don't let Meta consolidate all spend into the cheapest inventory
Choose optimization events that have the strongest correlation with paid subscription revenue
Test different optimization events and measure their impact on actual trial-to-paid conversion

title: Adding features won't fix trial CVR—better marketing will
insight: The myth is that adding product features will fix trial conversion rate. The reality is that better marketing is the primary lever. Trial CVR varies dramatically by traffic source and audience—differences of 27% to 52% are common. Don't evaluate UA solely on CPI or CPT. Instead measure incremental value and check first-party data for insights on which channels and demographics convert best.
raw_excerpt: Let's bust a Myth: Adding features won't fix your trial CVR. Better marketing will. The Easiest Way To Fix Trial CVR: 27%, 34%, 52%. 1: Traffic source and audience - Trial CVR differs massively by channel and audience. Don't just evaluate UA based on metrics like CPI or CPT. Measure incremental value and check 1P data for insights.
source_title: The Easiest Way To Fix Trial CVR
actionable_steps: Analyze trial CVR by traffic source, campaign, and creative to identify what's working
Collect channel and age data in onboarding (WDYHAU + age question) to model ROA by source
Stop optimizing purely on CPI/CPT—measure trial-to-paid conversion and LTV per channel
Prioritize marketing improvements over feature development for CVR gains

title: Creative messaging angle determines trial CVR—find your strongest angle first
insight: Creative is targeting, and targeting dictates value. Every app has multiple angles you can use to advertise it. Different angles attract different audiences with different willingness to pay and trial conversion rates. Find your strongest converting angle and nail that one first before diversifying. The messaging in your ads sets user expectations that directly impact whether they convert during trial.
raw_excerpt: 2: Creative messaging and angle. Creative is targeting. And targeting dictates value. Every app has several angles you can use to advertise it. Find your strongest converting angle and nail that one first.
source_title: The Easiest Way To Fix Trial CVR
actionable_steps: Map out all possible advertising angles for your app (pain points, benefits, use cases)
Test each angle in separate ad sets to measure trial CVR per angle
Double down on the highest-converting angle before testing new ones
Ensure ad messaging aligns with what users actually experience during trial

title: App Store page and paywall design are key CVR levers—use trust signals and clear pricing
insight: Your App Store page sets the stage for conversion. Making promises you can't keep hurts trial CVR. Instead, prime users with authority and trust signals—outcomes, university/institutional backing, social proof, and time-to-result claims. For paywall design: users who don't understand your offer will quickly cancel. High prices set expectations—not meeting them hurts CVR. Communicate clearly and price based on perceived value.
raw_excerpt: 3: App Store page and value prop - Just like ads, your store page sets the stage for conversion. Making big promises you can't keep? Trial conversion suffers. Key elements: Outcomes, Trust, Social Proof, Time-to-result. 5: Paywall design and price - Users who don't get your offer will quickly cancel. Communicate simple clear. Price on perceived value.
source_title: The Easiest Way To Fix Trial CVR
actionable_steps: Audit your App Store screenshots for trust signals: outcomes, social proof, institutional backing, time-to-result
Ensure ad messaging and store page messaging are aligned—no overpromising
Simplify paywall copy to clearly communicate the offer
Price based on perceived value, not just competitor pricing
Test different paywall designs with A/B testing to find the highest CVR version

title: Different creative types produce dramatically different age distributions
insight: A stacked bar chart across 10 different creative types shows that each creative format attracts a fundamentally different age composition. Some creatives skew heavily toward 18-24 year olds while others reach 45-65+ audiences. This is the visual proof that creative type IS targeting—by choosing your creative format, you're implicitly choosing your demographic mix. This is why ad sets should be separated by creative type rather than audience segment.
raw_excerpt: Age Composition by Creative Type [stacked bar chart showing age groups 18-24, 25-34, 35-44, 45-54, 55-64, 65+ across 10 different creative types with dramatically different distributions]
source_title: Age Composition by Creative Type (chart)
actionable_steps: Pull age breakdown reports per creative in your Meta Ads Manager
Map which of your creative types skew young vs old
If you need older (higher-LTV) audiences, invest more in creative formats that naturally reach them
Use this data to inform your ad set structure—separate by creative type to control age mix

title: Direct2App vs Web2App campaigns access completely different audience and placement buckets
insight: App Promotion (Direct2App) and Web Sales (Web2App) campaigns produce fundamentally different traffic profiles. App Promotion is Reels-heavy, skews young, makes static creatives hard to scale, and has lower AEM signal quality. Web Sales provides better FB Feed distribution, skews older, allows bid/cost caps to work reliably, and has high CAPI signal quality. Running both campaign types simultaneously lets you access both audience buckets and placement mixes.
raw_excerpt: Direct2App vs Web2App: Key differences — App Promotion: Reels-heavy, skews young, static creatives hard to scale, lower AEM signal quality. Web Sales: better FB Feed distribution, skews old, bid/cost caps work reliably, high CAPI signal quality. [Charts showing placement distribution over time and age composition over time for each]
source_title: Direct2App vs Web2App Traffic Composition (infographic)
actionable_steps: Run both App Promotion and Web Sales campaigns in parallel to access different audience buckets
Use Web Sales campaigns to reach older, higher-LTV audiences via FB Feed
Use Web Sales for reliable bid/cost cap performance and higher CAPI signal quality
Use App Promotion for Reels-heavy video creative scaling
Compare age composition and placement distribution between the two campaign types in your account

title: Use Value Rules with placement criteria to bid higher on valuable placements
insight: Meta's Value Rules feature allows you to set bid adjustments based on placement criteria (among other dimensions). The screenshot shows the Value Rules interface in Meta Ads Manager with the placement targeting option circled. This lets you tell Meta to bid more aggressively for conversions coming from specific placements that your data shows produce higher-LTV users, without restricting other placements entirely.
raw_excerpt: [Screenshot of Meta Ads Manager Value Rules interface showing 'Rule criteria' with placement option circled, demonstrating how to configure bid adjustments by placement]
source_title: Value Rules - Placement Criteria (Meta Ads Manager screenshot)
actionable_steps: Navigate to Value Rules in Meta Ads Manager (campaign level settings)
Add a rule with placement as the criteria dimension
Set higher value adjustments for placements that produce higher-LTV users in your data
Use Value Rules as an alternative to placement restrictions—guide Meta rather than restrict it
Monitor performance after implementing to ensure the bid adjustments are producing desired results
